rationale: In the citing paper's background section, Berger and Milkman (2012) is used for a specific prior result: content with higher emotional intensity and arousal is more likely to go viral. This is part of the conceptual setup for what drives social media engagement and helps justify the text-content controls used in the models. The citation therefore does more than a passing mention and meaningfully informs how the paper positions its contribution on image effects.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Berger and Milkman (2012) from primarily text-based virality drivers to visual content drivers, testing how image presence, image characteristics, and image-text fit affect engagement. It treats the cited paper as prior baseline evidence on sharing mechanisms while adding a new content modality and empirical settings.
